methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ultima Genomics is a Series C-stage biotech company currently valued at $4.0B post-money, having raised $700M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 8 private-equity-backed biotech firms at Series B through Series D stages, plus 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) in Biotech & Health.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $4.0B last post-money and applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—including Insitro ($2.4B Series C), HistoSonics ($2.2B Series D), and Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B Series B)—yields a median comparable valuation of $1.7B, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for the target.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $4.2B. The current $4.0B valuation aligns closely with the triangulated median. Key uncertainties stem from the wide dispersion in peer valuations and the stage-progression method's dependence on assumed multiplier stability. Revenue trajectory and competitive positioning within genomics remain material drivers not fully captured by comparable multiples alone.
